title: U.S. lifts blockade on Iranian ports as 60-day clock for a final deal starts ticking
description: The U.S. is allowing ships to enter and exit Iranian ports and coastal areas as the countries move to a new phase of negotiations over the next 60 days.

title: Student loan borrowers will get an interest rate cut if they sign up for auto pay
description: The Trump administration wants to jumpstart student loan repayment, with federal student loan debt approaching $2 trillion.

title: Read the full text of Trump's preliminary U.S.-Iran agreement to end the war
description: Here is the text of the memorandum of understanding that was signed Wednesday by President Trump and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian, as well as Pakistan's prime minister.

title: Ukraine hits a Moscow oil refinery and other sites in a large-scale drone attack
description: Ukraine launched a wave of drone attacks on Russia early Thursday — one of the largest attacks on Russia's capital since the Kremlin ordered the invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.

title: You're probably using too many skin care products. Here are the 3 essentials
description: We asked half a dozen skin care experts: Which products do you really need to keep your skin healthy and attractive? Here's what they said.

title: These 3 brothers lost their parents to AIDS. Now they struggle to make it on their own
description: Three brothers say their mother and father died after losing access to their HIV medications. Now the boys are figuring out how to navigate life.

title: Supreme Court sides with a marijuana user who was barred from owning guns
description: The court ruled that the law used to prosecute a marijuana user violated his Second Amendment right to bear arms and is unconstitutionally vague.

title: Embedded: "We Keep Us Safe" from NPR, KUOW and The Seattle Times
description: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.
